summary.goal: Deep integration with macOS to feel like a native citizen of the OS — file associations, Launch Services, appearance, and window lifecycle
summary.description:
Deep integration with macOS to feel like a native citizen of the OS.

## File Association
- Register as handler for markdown UTIs (.md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd)
- Appear in Finder "Open With" context menu
- User sets it as default viewer themselves (no first-run wizard)

## Open With Other App
- Uses macOS Launch Services API (NSWorkspace.open(_:withApplicationAt:) or NSWorkspace.urlsForApplications(toOpen:))
- Reads system-registered apps for the file type
- Presents list of associated applications
- No custom app detection — leverages OS-provided associations

## Appearance
- Follows system light/dark mode (NSAppearance)
- Automatic adaptation — no manual toggle
- Standard macOS window management
- Standard scroll behavior

## Window Lifecycle
- NSDocument-based architecture or lightweight equivalent
- Each file = new window
- Close last window = quit app
- No dock icon persistence when no windows open

acceptance_criteria.description: Appears in Finder Open With menu for markdown files
Open With functionality uses NSWorkspace APIs correctly
Respects NSAppearance changes for automatic theme switching
Proper window lifecycle with NSDocument or equivalent

observations: NSWorkspace APIs for Open With functionality
NSAppearance observation for automatic theme switching
No first-run wizard — user sets default viewer themselves
No dock icon persistence when no windows open

steps.title: Implement file change monitoring with reload banner
steps.description: Monitor the opened markdown file for external changes using DispatchSource.makeFileSystemObjectSource or FSEvents. When the file is modified externally (e.g., user edits in VS Code), show a subtle banner at the top of the content area with text like 'File changed on disk' and a 'Reload' button. Do NOT auto-reload — require explicit user action. If the file is deleted while viewing, show an inline message with the file path and keep the window open with the last-known content.

acceptance_criteria.description: File changes detected via DispatchSource or FSEvents
Subtle banner appears at top with 'Reload' button on change
No auto-reload — user must click Reload
File deletion shows inline message, window stays open
Reload re-reads file and re-renders through pipeline

architecture.banner_style: Thin bar at top of scroll view, theme-aware colors, dismissible
architecture.deletion: Show centered inline message with file path in muted text

design.reload_button: Text button with theme link color

steps.title: Implement NSDocumentController integration for Open Recent
steps.description: Integrate with NSDocumentController so macOS automatically tracks recently opened files. When a file is opened, call NSDocumentController.shared.noteNewRecentDocumentURL(fileURL). This populates the File > Open Recent submenu automatically. No custom recent-files tracking needed — leverage the system. NSDocumentController also handles the application(_:open:) delegate for file open events if configured correctly.

acceptance_criteria.description: Opened files appear in File > Open Recent submenu
Selecting a recent file opens it in a new window
Recent files persist across app launches (system managed)

architecture.menu: NSDocumentController automatically manages Open Recent submenu
architecture.note: Does not require NSDocument subclass — just noteNewRecentDocumentURL

steps.title: Configure app termination behavior and dock icon
steps.description: Configure the app to quit when the last window closes. Implement applicationShouldTerminateAfterLastWindowClosed() returning true in AppDelegate. Ensure the dock icon does not persist when all windows are closed (app terminates). Also handle the case where the app is launched without a file (e.g., user launches from Applications): show the Open File dialog (Cmd+O) immediately so the user can pick a file.

acceptance_criteria.description: App quits when last window is closed
No dock icon persists after last window close
Launching without a file shows Open File dialog

architecture.no_file_launch: applicationDidFinishLaunching checks if file URL received; if not, show NSOpenPanel
architecture.dock: App terminates so dock icon disappears naturally
